heading: Alexandra Bridge vs Alfred Cove — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Alexandra Bridge, Western Australia and Alfred Cove, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Alexandra Bridge has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Alfred Cove (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Alexandra Bridge or Alfred Cove safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Alexandra Bridge has a flood risk score of 50/100, while Alfred Cove scores 45/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Alexandra Bridge scores 28/100 for bushfire risk. Alfred Cove scores 59/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Alexandra Bridge or Alfred Cove?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Alexandra Bridge has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.65% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
